summary.narrative: Governor Lombardo has delivered on several major budget and education funding promises, including record K-12 investments and raises for teachers and law enforcement. He has maintained his pledge against new taxes while pursuing housing affordability initiatives. Some criminal justice and regulatory reform efforts remain ongoing.

promises.text: Pledge $2 billion per biennium for K-12 education with a 22% increase from the previous biennium
promises.evidence: Lombardo's budget delivered more than $2 billion per biennium for K-12 education, representing a 22%+ increase as outlined in his State of the State address and passed by the legislature.
Lombardo successfully enacted teacher pay raises as part of his education funding package, which he highlighted during his re-election campaign launch.
Lombardo pledged during his 2021 gubernatorial campaign announcement to veto new taxes and has maintained this position, vetoing tax increase legislation that reached his desk.

promises.text: Restore COVID-era budget cuts to higher education
promises.evidence: The governor's budget restored more than $75 million in COVID-era cuts to higher education and boosted graduate student stipends, marking the largest budget increase since 2019.

promises.text: Provide raises for law enforcement officers
promises.evidence: Lombardo delivered on his promise to increase law enforcement compensation, citing this achievement during his re-election announcement.
Lombardo has pursued efforts to increase attainable housing statewide, though the full impact of these initiatives is still being measured as programs roll out.
Lombardo enacted accountability measures for schools as part of his education reform agenda, with implementation ongoing across Nevada school districts.

promises.text: Take a strong law and order approach to reduce crime
promises.evidence: While Lombardo campaigned on a 'law and order' platform as former Clark County Sheriff, crime rates in Nevada have shown mixed results and some legislative priorities in this area were not fully enacted.
